summary: Cathy O'Neil announces receiving the PrivCo Spotlight Award from the Special Libraries Association of New York. The award recognizes bloggers, website founders, and those working outside traditional library roles. She was chosen for her work as a data scientist, her MathBabe blog, and her National Book Award-nominated book Weapons of Math Destruction. O'Neil expresses genuine enthusiasm about the honor, particularly because of her admiration for librarians.
key_points: O'Neil received the SLA-NY PrivCo Spotlight Award for her data science work and MathBabe blog
Weapons of Math Destruction was nominated for the 2016 National Book Award for Nonfiction
She views librarians as 'the original ethical data scientists'
The award celebrates those working outside traditional library and SLA-NY scope
money_quotes: I'm a huge fan of librarians – they are, in my opinion, the original ethical data scientists.
key_insight: Librarians represent an ethical model for data work that modern data scientists should aspire to emulate.
